output.final_deliverable:
Chain of Wikipedia URLs (start → end):
1) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
2) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Featured_article
3) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence
4) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning
5) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neural_network

Verified adjacency (extracted from visited page showing the link):
From URL[4] to URL[5]: the Machine learning page contains a link to Neural network. Evidence: the Machine learning page includes the link “Neural network” pointing to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neural_network (see visit) [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning].

Detailed steps and citations:
- I started at the Wikipedia Main Page: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page].
- From that page I followed the “Featured article” link to: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Featured_article [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Featured_article].
- From the Featured article page I followed the link to the article on Artificial intelligence: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence].
- From the Artificial intelligence article I followed the link to Machine learning: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning].
- From the Machine learning article I followed the link to Neural network: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neural_network [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neural_network].

Summary: I built a chain of five Wikipedia pages by starting at the Main Page and following in-page links: Main Page → Featured article → Artificial intelligence → Machine learning → Neural network. I verified the adjacency from Machine learning to Neural network by extracting the link from the Machine learning page to the Neural network URL (see citation above).
output.action_summary: Chain of 5 Wikipedia pages starting at Main Page, with one verified adjacency: Machine learning links to Neural network. All pages visited and cited: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Featured_article; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neural_network.

70e61941-92de-49b0-a901-92ad5e4eccbb.title:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

details.mandate:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

516d615c-d83c-40a4-add9-67f60764c313.title: Sequential plan: visit Wikipedia Main Page, follow links to build a 4+ URL chain, and verify one adjacency

details.objective: Produce an ordered chain of at least 4 Wikipedia article URLs where each URL after the first is a link found on the previous page. Also produce one verified adjacency near the end showing that URL[i] links to URL[i+1] (include evidence/snippet and source).

measurable_outputs.chain: Ordered array of >=4 full Wikipedia URLs (string list).

adjacency_verification.evidence_snippet: Text or HTML anchor text captured from the 'from_url' page that shows a link to 'to_url'.

plan_steps.intent: Extract internal Wikipedia article links present on the Main Page; collect candidate article URLs (title + href).
plan_steps.expected_output: List of internal wiki links (URLs and link text) found on the Main Page. This reduces uncertainty by providing concrete candidates to follow.

plan_steps.intent: Select a promising internal article link from step 1 (one likely to have further Wikipedia links) and visit it. Extract its internal article links.
plan_steps.selection_criteria: Prefer high-level articles (e.g., countries, historical events, major concepts) because they usually contain many internal links.
plan_steps.expected_output: Chosen URL_2 and list of internal links on URL_2.

plan_steps.intent: Repeat: pick a link from the previous visited page and visit it, extracting links, until the chain reaches at least 4 URLs (including the Main Page).
plan_steps.loop_details: Perform visits and extractions sequentially so each chosen URL is provably present on the previous page. Stop when chain length >= 4 (prefer 4–6).
plan_steps.expected_output: Ordered chain of URLs [URL_1 (Main Page), URL_2, URL_3, URL_4, ...].

plan_steps.intent: Select one adjacency pair near the end of the chain (preferably URL_{n-1} -> URL_n) and re-visit URL_{n-1} to extract an explicit link (anchor href or visible snippet) that points to URL_n. Capture the exact anchor text or surrounding sentence as evidence.
plan_steps.expected_output: adjacency_verification object (from_url, to_url, evidence_snippet, source_visited_url). This provides verifiable proof that the chain obeys the adjacency rule.

plan_steps.intent: Return the ordered chain and the adjacency verification object, citing the visited URLs used as evidence.
plan_steps.expected_output: Final answer payload containing the chain array and the adjacency verification with cited visited URL(s).

details.why_this_reduces_uncertainty: By visiting each page we obtain authoritative page content and link lists (not just search snippets). Re-visiting the 'from' page to capture the exact anchor that points to the 'to' page provides concrete evidence for the adjacency requirement.

notes_on_tools_and_rules.visit_rule: Start URL is explicitly provided, so visit it directly (no search). For subsequent pages, use visit to read full page content and extract links. Do not rely on search snippets for link verification.
notes_on_tools_and_rules.execution_mode: SEQUENTIAL (execute_all_children=false) because each chosen next URL must be confirmed from the previously visited page before proceeding.

details.parent_goal:

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.
